concerns.text: FR-007 ('System MUST ensure all statistical computations are performed using CPU-only methods...') anchors to 'See US-3'. However, US-3 (Statistical Analysis) focuses exclusively on the execution of t-tests and Mann-Whitney U tests. It contains no scope, acceptance criteria, or independent test regarding hardware constraints, runtime limits, or memory usage. The constraint is a non-functional requirement that is not covered by the functional scope of US-3, creating a silent scope creep where the requirement exists without a corresponding user story to justify or define its acceptance.

SC-005 ('The computational feasibility is measured against the free-tier CI constraints...') anchors to 'See FR-007'. Since FR-007 is not anchored to a valid User Story (as noted in the previous concern), SC-005 inherits this orphan status. There is no User Story (US-1, US-2, or US-3) that defines the goal of 'computational feasibility' or 'CI constraints' as a user need. This success criterion validates a constraint that has no backing story.

The Edge Case regarding 'DATASET_INSUFFICIENT' error (raising error if <50 posts) describes a specific validation logic and error code. While this relates to US-1 (Stimulus Curation), there is no explicit Functional Requirement (FR) that mandates the system to 'raise a DATASET_INSUFFICIENT error' or 'verify the raw dataset contains at least 50 unique posts' as a distinct requirement. FR-001 only mentions filtering for high-outrage posts. The specific error handling logic in the Edge Case is not explicitly captured in the FR list, leaving this critical failure mode without a formal requirement anchor.
